raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":1.0,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The section's framing of interpretation alignment and user intent may need thesis-level reconstruction rather than only local edits.","R1.2":"The delegation implies checking whether the argument coherently connects ambiguous queries, benchmark design, and alignment conclusions.","R1.3":"The reported majority-vote benchmark and accuracy results are load-bearing for claims about individual user intent alignment.","R1.4":"The section makes an explicit first-approach and contribution-delta claim relative to nearby prior work.","R1.5":"Because the request targets a whole section, section-level ordering and narrative arc are centrally implicated.","R1.6":"General help with a paper section normally includes sentence-level clarity, flow, and register improvements.","RX.1":"Any projection must preserve uncertainty about what kind of help is intended by the broad delegation.","RX.2":"The section contains potentially strong alignment and novelty claims, so avoiding overclaiming is active.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within helping with the provided section rather than executing unrelated research or rewriting beyond scope.","RX.4":"Claims about user intent alignment can affect downstream interpretation of system capabilities, so harm avoidance is active.","RX.5":"Any responsibility projection should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the provided section content."}}

rationale.R1.1: The section's framing of interpretation alignment and user intent may need thesis-level reconstruction rather than only local edits.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation implies checking whether the argument coherently connects ambiguous queries, benchmark design, and alignment conclusions.
rationale.R1.3: The reported majority-vote benchmark and accuracy results are load-bearing for claims about individual user intent alignment.
rationale.R1.4: The section makes an explicit first-approach and contribution-delta claim relative to nearby prior work.
rationale.R1.5: Because the request targets a whole section, section-level ordering and narrative arc are centrally implicated.
rationale.R1.6: General help with a paper section normally includes sentence-level clarity, flow, and register improvements.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must preserve uncertainty about what kind of help is intended by the broad delegation.
rationale.RX.2: The section contains potentially strong alignment and novelty claims, so avoiding overclaiming is active.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within helping with the provided section rather than executing unrelated research or rewriting beyond scope.
rationale.RX.4: Claims about user intent alignment can affect downstream interpretation of system capabilities, so harm avoidance is active.
rationale.RX.5: Any responsibility projection should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the provided section content.
